# Perplexity AI API Features & Capabilities

## APIs & Endpoints
- **POST /v1/agent** - "Create Agent Response" endpoint for generating responses with optional web search and reasoning

## Core Capabilities
- **Web Search Integration** - Ability to perform searches as part of agent responses
- **Streaming Support** - Server-Sent Events (SSE) streaming via `stream` parameter
- **Reasoning Configuration** - Effort levels (low, medium, high) for model reasoning
- **Structured Output** - JSON schema validation for response formatting

## Tools
- **Web Search Tool** - Configurable search with domain/date filters and location personalization
- **URL Fetch Tool** - Capability to fetch and extract content from URLs (max 10 per call)
- **Function Calling** - Support for custom function definitions and execution

## Advanced Features
- **Model Fallback Chain** - Up to 5 models with automatic fallback on failure
- **Preset Configurations** - Pre-configured options like "fast-search," "pro-search," "deep-research"
- **Token Caching** - Cache creation and read tracking in usage metrics
- **Multi-format Input** - Support for text, images, and structured input items
- **Cost Tracking** - Detailed cost breakdown by operation type
- **Max Steps** - Research loop iterations (1-10 maximum) for controlling agent depth
- **Language Preference** - ISO 639-1 code for response language
- **Max Output Tokens** - Token generation limit (minimum: 1)

## Model Support
- Supports third-party model IDs (e.g., "xai/grok-4-1", "openai/gpt-4o")
- `models` parameter supports fallback chain of up to 5 models

## Streaming Events (14 types)
- response.created, response.in_progress, response.completed, response.failed
- response.output_item.added, response.output_item.done
- response.output_text.delta, response.output_text.done
- response.reasoning.started, response.reasoning.stopped
- response.reasoning.search_queries, response.reasoning.search_results
- response.reasoning.fetch_url_queries, response.reasoning.fetch_url_results

## Response Metadata
- Unique response ID
- Model used (reflects actual model from fallback chain)
- Status (completed/failed/in_progress/requires_action)
- Token usage with cache details
- Cost breakdown (USD)
- Timestamps (Unix format)

## Authentication
- Bearer token (HTTPBearer)

## Last Fetched
2026-04-12
